content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95314

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660 322-5601 322-6441

Mr. Richard Boyle
c/o "Boyle for Senator Campaign"
554 Broadway
San Francisco, Ca. 94133

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 76-030

Dear Mr. Boyle:

Thank you for your letter of March 1, 1976, requesting
an opinion with respect to the disclosure of a candidate's
political debts and related obligations.

Government Code Section 83114 provides that "any person
may request the Commission to issue an opinion with respect
to his duties under the [Act]." Pursuant to this statutory
authorization, regulations adopted by the Commission require
that requests for opinions be submitted by the person whose
duties are in question or by that person's representative.
Your request poses a question concerning the duties of Robert
H. Mendelsohn, not your own duties. For these reasons, no
formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope that the
following informal comments will be helpful.

Candidates and committees disclose their financial
conditions on campaign statements filed pursuant to appli-
cable provisions of the Political Reform Act. However, the
Act does not require disclosure of a debt existing at the
time when it took effect on January 7, 1975.

If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West,
an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman,
For the Commission

DHL:pvp

WESTERN UNION MAILGRAM

[Various stamps and reference numbers at top]

VIA 2 USPM'76

MR. DANIEL M. LOEWENSTEIN, CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 K STREET
SACRAMENTO CA 95614


DEAR MR. LOEWENSTEIN:

I AM A CANDIDATE FOR STATE SENATOR AS A DEMOCRATIC NOMINEE
IN THE FIFTH SENATE DISTRICT IN THE JUNE 8, 1976 PRIMARY ELECTION.

IN THIS CONNECTION I AM REQUESTING AN OPINION OF THE
COMMISSION ON A MATTER WHICH I CONSIDER TO BE OF CRUCIAL URGENCY. THE
QUESTION IS

---ARE THERE ANY PROVISIONS WITHIN THE
POLITICAL REFORM ACT OF 1974 OR THE
COMMISSION'S REGULATIONS, GUIDELINES
AND RULINGS, EXPLICIT OR IMPLIED,
WHICH WOULD EXEMPT A CANDIDATE FOR
STATE OFFICE FROM DISCLOSING HIS
CURRENT POLITICAL DEBTS AND/OR
RELATED OBLIGATIONS ?

I RECOGNIZE THAT THE COMMISSION DOES NOT CONSIDER
HYPOTHETICAL QUESTIONS. MY QUESTION RELATES SPECIFICALLY TO REPORTS
WHICH HAVE APPEARED IN THE PRESS AND ELSEWHERE TO THE EFFECT THAT
ANOTHER CANDIDATE IN THE ELECTION REFERRED TO, ROBERT H. NENDELSOHN,
MAY HAVE OUTSTANDING POLITICAL DEBTS AND OTHER POLITICAL OBLIGATIONS
TOTALING SOME $150,000.

PLEASE UNDERSTAND THAT I AM IN NO WAY QUESTIONING MR.
MENDELSSOHN'S INTEGRITY OR HONESTY. I AM MERELY SEEKING AN OPINION OF
THE COMMISSION WITH RESPECT TO THE QUESTION WHICH I RAISED.

I RECOGNIZE, ALSO, THAT THE COMMISSION'S NORMAL PROCEDURES
COULD RESULT IN A DELAY IN ITS CONSIDERING THE QUESTION.

I TRUST, HOWEVER, THAT THE COMMISSION WILL APPRECIATE THE
TIMELY URGENCY AND SIGNIFICANCE OF THE QUESTION AND ACCORD IT THE
HIGHEST POSSIBLE PRIORITY CONSIDERATION CONSISTENT WITH THE INTENT OF
THE ACT.

PLS

MITTE

LY,

RICHARD BOYLE
C/O "BOYLE FOR SENATOR CAMPAIGN"
554 BROADWAY
SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94133
(TELEPHONE: 415-397-7433)

P.S. WOULD YOU PLEASE SEND ME IMMEDIATELY COPIES OF ALL INFORMATION
WHICH MAY BE AVAILABLE RELATING TO THE ACT--COMMISSION'S REGULATIONS,
GUIDELINES, RULINGS ETCETERA.

02:25 EST

MONSACT H88

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the Fair Political Practices Commission of California, dated March 16, 1976, addressed to Mr. Richard Boyle regarding his request for an opinion about disclosure of a candidate's political debts and related obligations. The letter states: --- State of California Fair Political Practices Commission P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 Technical Assistance/Administration • • • Executive Legal • • • Enforcement/Conflict of Interest (916) 322-5660 322-5921 322-6441 Mr. Richard Boyle c/o "Boyle for Senator Campaign" 554 Broadway San Francisco, Ca. 94133 Re: Request for Opinion No. 76-030 Dear Mr. Boyle: Thank you for your letter of March 1, 1976, requesting an opinion with respect to the disclosure of a candidate's political debts and related obligations. Government Code Section 83114 provides that "any person may request the Commission to issue an opinion with respect to his duties under the [Act]." Pursuant to this statutory authorization, regulations adopted by the Commission require that requests for opinions be submitted by the person whose duties are in question or by that person's representative. Your request poses a question concerning the duties of Robert H. Mendelsohn, not your own duties. For these reasons, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope that the following informal comments will be helpful. Candidates and committees disclose their financial conditions on campaign statements filed pursuant to applicable provisions of the Political Reform Act. However, the Act does not require disclosure of a debt existing at the time when it took effect on January 7, 1975. If you have any questions, please contact Natalie West, an attorney with the Legal Division of the Commission staff. Sincerely, [Signature] Daniel H. Lowenstein Chairman, For the Commission DHL:pvp The letter is addressed to Mr. Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman of the Fair Political Practices Commission, at 1100 K Street, Sacramento, CA 95814. The sender is seeking an opinion from the commission regarding whether there are any provisions within the Political Reform Act of 1974 or the commission's regulations, guidelines, and rulings that would exempt a candidate for state office from disclosing his current political debts and/or related obligations. The sender acknowledges that the commission does not consider hypothetical questions but specifically refers to reports in the press and elsewhere suggesting that another candidate in the election, Robert H. Mendelssohn, may have outstanding political debts totaling some $150,000. The sender states that they are not questioning Mr. Mendelssohn's integrity or honesty but are merely seeking an opinion on the commission's procedures with respect to the question raised. The sender also recognizes that the commission's normal procedures could result in a delay in considering the question. I trust, however, that the Commission will appreciate the timely urgency and significance of the question and accord it the highest possible priority consideration consistent with the intent of the Act. PL S MITR LY, RICHARD BOYLE C/O "BOYLE FOR SENATOR CAMPAIGN" 554 BROADWAY SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94133 (TELEPHONE: 415-397-7433) P.S. Would you please send me immediately copies
